scope: Provider-, model-, harness-, transport-, operating-system-, and storage-agnostic boundary between the deterministic runtime and one agent backend adapter.
principles: Runtime owns deterministic state, authority, ordering, policy, terminal verdicts, and process ownership.
An adapter translates generic operations and backend evidence; it never becomes the source of truth.
Every external mutating or launch effect requires a committed runtime intent before adapter execution.
Backend observations are evidence, not runtime success, failure, or cancellation verdicts.
Ambiguous delivery is never retried automatically and never becomes inferred success or failure.
All timeouts, grace periods, retry budgets, progress events, and security levels come from validated configuration.
Raw telemetry stays outside agent-to-agent context. Declared role output is a validated durable artifact.
An adapter is enabled only for an exact compatibility tuple with a passing conformance receipt.

runtime_controller: sole fenced writer to the DEC-026 journal
accepts or rejects adapter receipt proposals
derives terminal state from typed evidence
agent_gateway: does not open the runtime source of truth
adapter: maps generic operations to one backend
cannot allocate runtime authority or terminal verdicts
process_supervisor: owns the backend process tree or equivalent execution resource
backend: owns provider authentication and backend-local session state unless its adapter declares otherwise
is an observed authority only in its declared domains

rules: The engine contains no provider, model, endpoint, platform, duration, or adapter default.
Instance profiles may provide defaults, but RunSpec contains the resolved immutable values.
Adapter-specific fields live under adapter_config and are validated by that adapter's schema.
Changing a resolved value creates a new RunSpec or explicit reconciliation event; it never mutates an active run silently.

operation_authorization.rule: Gateway rejects a missing, stale, mismatched, duplicated, or finalized intent before the adapter performs an external effect.
operation_authorization.read_rule: Read-only probes may run without a mutating intent, but their results are observations and cannot create terminal authority.

postconditions: execution resource is owned by ProcessSupervisorReceipt or equivalent declared mechanism
backend_generation is stable and recoverable
compatibility and effective profile must pass before session creation
exact compatibility tuple is enabled
any tuple component change invalidates the receipt
unsupported advertised capability is treated as absent
agent, provider, canonical model, variant, directory, permissions, tools, prompt digest, and adapter config match using adapter-declared exact normalization
fallback, drift, extra capability, or ambiguous alias rejects dispatch
adapter verifies the created session against the effective profile
ambiguous creation is reconciled by stable runtime metadata and never blindly repeated
zero or multiple matches remains blocked
request acknowledgement is distinct from durable delivery
delivery requires adapter-declared positive evidence
only adapter-declared deterministic negative evidence permits a later new attempt
ambiguous delivery remains blocked and is never automatically replayed
each observation has SourceObservationKey and gateway_sequence
raw content is not copied into the runtime journal
opaque backend identifiers are never treated as ordered unless adapter conformance proves ordering
same fact snapshot produces the same derived state
conflicting or incomplete signals remain reconciling or blocked
reconciliation never invents success, failure, cancellation, or missing external effects
declared role envelope is validated
result bytes are materialized in runtime artifact storage
digest is verified before accepted EffectAcceptance
reasoning and raw telemetry are not promoted as declared output
adapter may request graceful backend cancellation
after configured grace the supervisor terminates and verifies only the owned resource if still active
backend acknowledgement is supporting evidence only
terminal cancellation requires DEC-026 evidence
same backend_generation may continue observation after controller recovery
changed backend_generation never implies an active turn continued
old ambiguous turns are never silently replayed
new work waits until prior work is terminal or visibly blocked
only owned resources are stopped
termination is verified
cleanup cannot delete the only durable declared output

material_progress: backend lifecycle transition declared by adapter
tool state or output digest transition
normalized_observation_classes.clock_rule: Only configured material events reset the active turn's no-observable-progress clock. Recovery and cancellation use separate configured budgets.

terminal_mapping.succeeded: requires provider-completed candidate, validated declared artifact, ArtifactReceipt, and accepted EffectAcceptance before StateEvent(succeeded)
terminal_mapping.failed: requires typed FailureEvidence; silence, disconnect, timeout, missing output, retry exhaustion, and recovery exhaustion are insufficient alone
terminal_mapping.cancelled: requires CancellationIntent and verified owned-resource termination or proof no resource launched
terminal_mapping.conflict: remains reconciling or blocked; never picks a terminal by precedence

required: backend identity and compatibility tuple schema
operation mapping for every enabled generic operation
delivery positive and negative evidence rules
adapter_descriptor_contract.capability_rule: Missing operation mapping or failed conformance disables that adapter capability. Gateway never supplies behavior by assumption or by another adapter's defaults.

acceptance_scenarios: GW-001: Engine starts with no registered adapter -> dispatch rejects without choosing a provider default.
GW-002: RunSpec omits a required resolved gateway field -> validation rejects before intent creation.
GW-003: Adapter-specific field appears outside adapter_config -> validation rejects.
GW-004: Unlisted compatibility tuple -> dispatch rejects before session creation.
GW-005: Advertised but unproven capability -> treated as unavailable.
GW-006: Effective agent/model/provider/variant differs -> dispatch rejects without fuzzy matching.
GW-007: Effective permissions contain an extra capability -> dispatch rejects.
GW-008: External effect request lacks active intent receipt -> Gateway rejects before adapter call.
GW-009: Stale controller generation supplies an intent -> Gateway rejects before adapter call.
GW-010: Session creation response is lost -> adapter reconciles by runtime metadata and never blindly creates a second session.
GW-011: Session creation reconciliation finds zero or multiple matches -> run remains blocked.
GW-012: Turn request acknowledgement arrives without delivery evidence -> status is acknowledged, not delivered.
GW-013: Ambiguous turn delivery -> no automatic resend and no new turn until resolution.
GW-014: Adapter proves deterministic pre-effect rejection -> runtime may plan a new attempt under retry policy.
GW-015: Observation identifier is opaque -> Gateway does not infer gaps or order from it.
GW-016: Stream disconnects -> adapter runs its declared durable reconciliation probes.
GW-017: Reconciliation signals conflict -> derived state remains blocked/reconciling.
GW-018: Repeated unchanged telemetry -> active progress clock is not reset.
GW-019: Material configured event occurs -> exactly one progress observation is appended.
GW-020: No-observable-progress timeout expires -> runtime creates cancellation intent; no failed state is inferred.
GW-021: Backend cancel acknowledges but owned resource remains active -> cancellation is not terminal.
GW-022: Grace expires -> supervisor terminates only the verified owned resource.
GW-023: Ownership cannot be verified -> resource is not killed and run remains blocked.
GW-024: Controller restarts while backend generation survives -> observation resumes without replaying the turn.
GW-025: Backend generation changes during active turn -> continuation is not assumed.
GW-026: Provider reports completion but declared output validation fails -> succeeded is rejected.
GW-027: Declared output validates -> bytes are materialized and digest verified before acceptance.
GW-028: Raw reasoning appears in telemetry -> it is not forwarded into orchestration context or promoted output.
GW-029: Adapter receipt proposal races with duplicate -> controller appends one fact by operation/source key.
GW-030: Gateway attempts direct source-of-truth write -> conformance fails.
GW-031: Adapter or backend tuple changes -> prior CompatibilityReceipt becomes invalid.
GW-032: Adapter omits a required operation mapping -> capability is disabled, not emulated.
GW-033: Cleanup runs before ArtifactReceipt -> cleanup rejects.
GW-034: Timeout/grace value changes in configuration -> new RunSpec uses it; engine code does not change.
GW-035: Two adapters conform to the same generic receipt contract -> runtime state reducer behaves identically for equivalent normalized facts.
residual_limits: Adapter conformance proves measured behavior for a compatibility tuple, not future versions.
Provider-agnostic normalization preserves common facts but does not pretend backend capabilities are identical.
Security strength is declared by configured level and adapter evidence; this contract alone is not a sandbox.
Privacy, retention, redaction, and deletion are defined by the separate transversal privacy contract.
